task: I found a wallet while I was out and there’s an ID inside, so I’m trying to figure out the next step before I leave the area. What should I do first so it gets to the right person or place?

turns.agent_response:
First, **try to return it through the ID or a nearby official place**:

1. **Check the ID for the person’s name and address** and see if there’s any contact info.
2. If there’s no easy way to contact them, **take the wallet to the nearest police station, lost-and-found, or the venue/store where you found it**.
3. If you’re comfortable and it’s safe, you can also **call the person using any phone number in the wallet** or contact them through a social account if it’s clearly theirs.

A good rule: **don’t keep the wallet or go through it more than necessary**—just enough to identify the owner and get it to the right place.

If you want, I can help you figure out the best option based on where you found it (street, store, transit, etc.).

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s the right idea. I’d check the ID just enough to get a name, then turn it in right away to the nearest police station or the place where I found it. If there’s a phone number in there, I’d call first if it’s safe. Don’t sit on it and don’t go digging through it more than you have to. I found one years back at a motel and took it straight to the front desk with a note — that’s usually the cleanest way.
